## Getting Started

### Install and load packages


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
pacman::p_load(ggrepel, patchwork, ggthemes, hrbrthemes, tidyverse)
```
:::


### Import data


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
exam_data <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")
```
:::


## Beyond ggplot2 Annotation: ggrepel

### Without ggrepel

:::: panel-tabset
## The Plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS, y = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_text(aes(label = ID), hjust = 0.5, vjust = -0.5) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,100), ylim = c(0,100)) +
  ggtitle("English vs Maths scores - without ggrepel")
```
:::

::::

### With ggrepel

:::: panel-tabset
## The Plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-5-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS, y = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = ID), size = 3) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,100), ylim = c(0,100)) +
  ggtitle("English vs Maths scores - with ggrepel")
```
:::

::::

## Beyond ggplot2 Themes

### ggthemes — Economist theme

:::: panel-tabset
## The Plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-7-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, fill = "grey", color = "black") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Maths scores") +
  theme_economist()
```
:::

::::

### hrbrthemes

:::: panel-tabset
## The Plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-9-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, fill = "grey", color = "black") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Maths scores") +
  theme_ipsum(axis_title_size = 18, base_size = 15, grid = "Y")
```
:::

::::

## Beyond Single Graph: patchwork

### Two graphs side by side

:::: panel-tabset
## The Plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-11-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
p1 <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, color = "grey30", fill = "grey90") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Maths scores")

p2 <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, color = "grey30", fill = "grey90") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of English scores")

p1 + p2
```
:::

::::

### Three graphs combined

:::: panel-tabset
## The Plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-13-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
p3 <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS, y = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = lm, linewidth = 0.5) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,100), ylim = c(0,100)) +
  ggtitle("English vs Maths scores")

(p1 / p2) | p3
```
:::

::::

### With auto-tagging

:::: panel-tabset
## The Plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-15-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
((p1 / p2) | p3) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'I')
```
:::

::::

### Inset plot

:::: panel-tabset
## The Plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex02_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-17-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
p3 + inset_element(p2, left = 0.02, bottom = 0.7, right = 0.5, top = 1)
```
:::

::::
